text:
**Two independent publish call-sites**, both going through this same codec:

- **Per-session engine** — `src/runtime.rs`. `status_for()` (`runtime.rs:97-114`) builds `domain::Status` from the live `Session` row; `enqueue_status()` (`runtime.rs:140-173`) encodes+signs it and pushes the signed JSON onto a SQLite `outbox` table (never talks to the relay directly). `spawn_outbox_drainer()` (`src/daemon/server/status_publish.rs:125-170`) drains that outbox and calls `state.transport.publish_event_checked`.
- **Daemon-wide heartbeat re-armer** — `spawn_status_heartbeat_publisher()` (`src/daemon/server/status_publish.rs:76-118`). Runs every `HEARTBEAT_SECS` (30s, `src/domain.rs:17`) over *every* alive session in the store, builds `domain::Status` via `status_from_session()` (`status_publish.rs:10-29`) from the same `Session` row, and calls `state.provider.set_status()` directly (`src/fabric/provider.rs:231-256`) — bypassing the outbox entirely.

Both read `title`/`activity`/`working` off the same `sessions` DB row and both publish independently on their own ~30s timers (see failure mode in §4). [...] - **Two heartbeat cadences configurable independently and inconsistently.** `TENEX_EDGE_HEARTBEAT_MS` (per-engine) and the hardcoded `HEARTBEAT_SECS` constant (daemon-wide re-armer) are not the same knob — an operator tuning one believes they've changed status refresh cadence but the other loop still fires on the default.
